source: Immutable-protocol facts seeded as verified; upgradeable protocols are populated from on-chain events via Sieve (see sieve/README.md). Refresh with `npm run export:timeline`.

liquity-v1.detail: Liquity V1 core contracts are immutable with no admin key or human governance. No upgrades or admin actions are possible after deploy — an empty timeline here is itself the signal.

liquity-v2.detail: Liquity V2 (BOLD) core contracts are immutable. LQTY-staked governance routes PIL liquidity incentives only, never protocol control — so there are no upgrade or admin actions to record.

morpho.detail: Morpho Blue is a directly-deployed singleton — no proxy, no implementation slot, no upgrade path. Governance can only whitelist IRMs/LLTVs (additive, irreversible) and set a capped fee; it cannot upgrade, pause, or alter existing markets.

uniswap-v3.detail: Uniswap v3 core (factory + pools) is non-upgradeable. Governance's only core power is the factory owner role (enable fee tiers / transfer owner); it cannot change swap or AMM logic.

uniswap-v4.detail: Uniswap v4's singleton PoolManager is non-upgradeable. Governance's only core power is the protocol-fee switch; per-pool hooks are deployed permissionlessly and are not protocol-level upgrades.
